semanticCore: Gushing forth, flowing, bursting through a crack, or water splitting open a rock

keyDerivations.meaning: To gush out, burst forth, split open and flow (water from stone)

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأَوْحَيْنَا إِلَىٰ مُوسَىٰ إِذِ اسْتَسْقَاهُ قَوْمُهُ أَنِ اضْرِبْ بِعَصَاكَ الْحَجَرَ ۖ فَانْبَجَسَتْ مِنْهُ اثْنَتَا عَشْرَةَ عَيْنًا (7:160): We inspired Moses... 'Strike the stone with your staff.' And there gushed forth from it twelve springs.
summaryEn: The root represents water bursting forth through rock fissures (Bajs), detailing the miracle of twelve springs gushing from the struck stone to sustain the Hebrew tribes.
summaryAr: يمثل هذا الجذر خروج الماء وانفجاره من ضيق كالشقوق والثقوب في الصخر (بجس)، واصفاً انبجاس العيون الاثنتي عشرة من الحجر استجابة لضربة عصا موسى.
rawText: انبجس 7 It (water) had a way, passage, vent, or channel, opened for it to flow forth; it had vent; it poured forth; (S, A, Msb, K;) [it burst forth;] from a cloud or clouds, and from a spring; (A;) and from a rock; (Kur vii. 160;) as also 🡻بَجَسَ, aor. ﹹ; (S, TA;) and 🡻تبجّس: (S, K:) syn. of the first, (S, A, K, * TA,) and last, (S,) اِنْفَجَرَ: (S, A, TA:) or of the last, تَفَجَّرَ [properly signifying it poured forth copiously]: (A, TA:) اِنْبِجَاسٌ signifies particularly the welling forth [of water] from a spring: or it has a general application: (K:) and 🡻بَجْسٌ signifies cracking in a water-skin, or stone, or earth, so that water issues from it. (TA.) You say, السَّحَابُ يَنْبَجِسُ بِالمَطَرِ [The clouds pour with rain]. (TA.) And 🡻يَتَبَجَّسُأَتَانَا بِثَرِيدٍ, (A,) or 🡻يَتَبَجَّسُأُدْمًا, (TA,) [He brought us crumbled bread moistened with broth, which streamed with seasoning,] meaning, by reason of the abundance of grease [in it]. (A, TA.) بَاجِسٌ ; pl. بُجَّسٌ: see بَجْسٌ. بَجيسٌ : see بَجْسٌ, in two places. بَجَسَ المَآءَ 1 , (S, A, Msb, K,) aor. ﹹ (S, Msb, K) and ﹻ, (A, K,) inf. n. بَجْسٌ, (Msb, TA,) He opened a way, passage, vent, or channel, for the water to flow forth; gave vent to it; made it to flow; syn. فَجَرَهُ, (S,) or فَتَحَهُ, (Msb,) or شَقَّهُ: (A, K:) [all of which, in this case, signify the same:] and in like manner one says of a wound; (A, K;) but in this case, the phrase is tropical: (TA:) and 🡻بجّسالَمآءِ, inf. n. تَبْجِيسٌ, He (namely, God, TA) made the water to flow forth, or to flow forth copiously, syn. فَجَّرَهُ, (K, TA,) from the cloud or clouds, and from the spring. (TA.) ___ See also 7, in two places. بَجَّسَ 2 see 1. تَبَجَّسَ 5 see 7, in three places. مَآءٌ بَجْسٌ Water having a way, passage, vent, or channel, opened for it to flow forth; having a vent; or pouring forth: (K:) and in like manner, سَحَابٌ بَجْسٌ [clouds pouring forth rain]; (TA;) and [so] سَحَائِبُ بُجَّسٌ [pl. of 🡻بَاجِسٌ and بَاجِسَةٌ]: (S:) and مَآءٌ 🡻بَجِيسٌ flowing water: (Kr, TA:) and عَيْنٌ 🡻بَجِيسٌ a copious spring. (K, * TA.)
